conditions.critical_condition_basis: critical point of adsorption for PEG
conditions.evidence_text: Typical LC-CC conditions for PEO are a non-polar stationary phase of RP-18 (octadecyl modified silica gel) and a mobile phase composition of methanol-water 81:19 wt.-%. At critical conditions, PEGs of different molar masses elute at the same elution volume.
conditions.notes: Used for analysis of MPEG, MPEG-MM, and copolymers containing PEO grafts.
conditions.corresponding_author_name: Frank Rittig; Stefan Becker; Harald Pasch
conditions.physical_address: BASF Aktiengesellschaft, Polymer Research, 67056 Ludwigshafen, Germany; Deutsches Kunststoff-Institut (German Institute for Polymers), Schloßgartenstr. 6, 64289 Darmstadt, Germany

conditions.analyte_polymer: copolymer of MPEG-MM 2 and methacrylic acid (sample 2)
conditions.critical_condition_basis: critical point of adsorption for PEO
conditions.evidence_text: Again, critical conditions for PEG are used to compensate for the effect of the PEO molar mass distribution.
conditions.notes: Used for analysis of copolymers containing PEO grafts.
